_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Ability to distinguish between problems that require machine learning and problems that can be solved more simply and reliably with rule-based logic or statistical methods.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Ability AI-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-003 (TF, cosine: 0.553), TF-A-001 (TF, cosine: 0.541).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.3/10 (raw 0.428). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [identifying, requirements, skill] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, based, distinguish, learning, logic, machine] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.878 (P=0.912, R=0.847). P exceeds R by 0.066 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.0%). "Skill in identifying requirements" and "Ability to distinguish between problems that require machine learning and proble" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.565 > STS 0.428 (gap +0.136). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [identifying, requirements, skill]. WIDAI terms: [ability, based, distinguish, learning, logic, machine].
